issue_title: Google PubSub Subscription stops pulling messages from topic.
issue_body:
In response to issue [5444 - Pubsub 0.35.3 stop consuming messages](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/issues/5444#issuecomment-400500096) and per theacodes request I am opening up a new issue.

I am currently running a container on kubernetes with 40 pods that are pulling from a subscription that is receiving ~50 messages a second. I am currently running 35.4

This is my code that i modified from the documentation that is pulling the messages.

```
def main():
    #Receives messages from a pull subscription.
    subscriber = pubsub_v1.SubscriberClient()
    subscription_path = subscriber.subscription_path(project, subscription_name)

    def callback(message):
        log.info('Received message: {}'.format(message))
        try:
            ret = bl.process_message(message)
            if ret.status_code == 200:
                log.info('200 File processed')
                statsd.increment('data-log-file-processed')
                message.ack()
            else:
                log.error('Could not process message for data log file publisher. {}' .format(ret.message))
                message.ack()
        except Exception as e:
            log.error('Could not process message: {} error occurred. {}' .format(ret.message, e))
    subscriber.subscribe(subscription_path, callback=callback)

    # The subscriber is non-blocking, so we must keep the main thread from
    # exiting to allow it to process messages in the background.
    log.info('Listening for messages on {}'.format(subscription_path))
    while True:
        time.sleep(60)
```

The SubsciberClient will pull messages for a time than just stop pulling them. After putting the logs to debug mode i am seeing the following:

[Log File](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/11DnGk_HchcCwOH_vKnd_UnZqs25qWAT4nDqoyDQjdqk/edit?usp=sharing)

Line 97 is the last message I received after that it seems to just go into an endless loop without ever pulling any messages. I know there are more messages to be pulled because my other pods are still pulling messages from the topic.

Please let me know if there is anything else needed to get this fixed or if it is something I am doing wrong. Thanks!

issue_title: Dataset ID aliases don't behave consistently.
issue_body:
Using the "human-readable" version `foo` of the prefixed ID `s~foo` (or `e~foo`) is allowed in a URI but not in a request payload (and will never be returned in a response since `foo` gets mapped to `s~foo` in the response).

This inconsistency can catch user's off-guard and requires custom behavior in `to_protobuf()`-type methods which essentially always ignore the `dataset_id` on a `PartitionId` message and only use the dataset ID in the URI.

/cc @silvolu @tseaver

----------------------------

Some examples

1.  Sending a query to

    ```
    https://www.googleapis.com/datastore/v1beta2/datasets/foo/runQuery
    ```

    which has `RunQueryRequest.partition_id.dataset_id == 'foo'` in the payload results in

    ```
    app s~foo cannot access app foo's data
    ```

1.  Sending a mutation (or a lookup) to one of

    ```
    https://www.googleapis.com/datastore/v1beta2/datasets/foo/allocateIds
    https://www.googleapis.com/datastore/v1beta2/datasets/foo/commit
    https://www.googleapis.com/datastore/v1beta2/datasets/foo/lookup
    ```

    which has `Key.partition_id.dataset_id == 'foo'` (either directly in a key
    or in an `Entity`) in the payload results in

    ```
    app s~foo cannot access app foo's data
    ```

1.  Sending a filtered or ancestor query which does not have
    `RunQueryRequest.partition_id.dataset_id` set in the payload but does have
    the dataset ID set in either the ancestor key

    ```
    The query app is 'foo' but ancestor.key app is 'foo'.
    ```

    or in the `__key__` filter

    ```
    __key__ filter app is foo but query app is s~foo
    ```

issue_title: v1beta3 returns Text fields as Strings and doesn't populate Meaning field to distinguish between them
issue_body:
AppEngine Datastore supports a Text object type:
https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/java/datastore/entities#Java_Properties_and_value_types

but Cloud Datastore does not:
https://cloud.google.com/datastore/docs/concepts/entities#properties_and_value_types

As a result, when an Entity with a Text property is inserted from AppEngine, it cannot be properly manipulated from the Cloud Datastore API because com.google.datastore.v1beta3.Value.getMeaning() always returns zero so we can't distinguish a Text object from a String.

Unless I'm missing something, this makes the Cloud Datastore API unusable for those interoperating with the AppEngine Datastore API.

issue_title: Emulator Env Variable setting not persisting
issue_body:
Hi, I decided to open this issue as I couldn't find anything related to this problem. This might be a very specific problem so I hope if nothing else this will serve as documentation for future devs. 

I am running my emulator on a Windows Server R2 VM, the issue I was having is that my code was creating the entities on the actual datastore and not on the emulator, even though the emulator were running and the variables were being set (I thought). 

Ok, so the command to automatically set the env variables is 

`gcloud beta emulators datastore env-init > set_vars.cmd && set_vars.cmd`

What it does is:  `gcloud beta emulators datastore env-init` contain the actual commands like `set DATASTORE_EMULATOR_HOST=XXXX` , that information is then piped to a file called set_vars.cmd and then this file is ran.

The problem start when you decide to put this command on a BAT or CMD file, it simply doesn't work. I tried a lot of things, including running it as a PS1 file with unrestricted execution policy, to no avail.

Alright, I can live without automatically setting this in a bat file, let me manually run the commands in set_vars.cmd. At first it seems to work, the prompt say it was set, you call an ECHO and it show you the variable's value but when you close the command prompt and open it again, the variables are not there.

After some research, the way I found to make these sets persist are using SETX like `setx DATASTORE_EMULATOR_HOST localhost:8081` . Now, after you issue this command, ECHO won't show you anything on the command prompt, you have to close it an open another one and the variable will be there (This is not necessary to set the variable, just to see it is there). Alternatively you can go to the UI for setting env vars and you will see it there, with a small catch, SETX only set user variables, not System variables but this don't seem to interfere with the emulator usage.

Also, the cherry on top, because a single problem was no enough, if you are using IntelliJ you need to close it and open again after setting the variables, yes... restart the IDE (not sure if this also happens with other IDEs).

To sum up, assuming I am doing something wrong and it would be possible to set this variables through a BAT or CMD file, it would be useful if `gcloud beta emulators datastore env-init` had an option of outputting the commands using `SETX`

Thank you for your support.

issue_title: Better result reporting
issue_body:
At the moment `import_data()` is a monolithic function with no easy customization points. In order to provide better feedback to users, I would like to extend it to report the total number of rows in each result type (NEW, UPDATE, SKIP, DELETE), including the total number of skipped rows even when `report_skipped == False`

There is no easy way to do this without copying the whole `import_data()` function into the subclass because there is no way to override Result class used, or the handling of individual rows.

I could achieve my requirements by making a slight change to import_data() to count the total number of each result type.

If it is desirable, I could also:

* replace the `result = Result()` with `result = self.get_result_class()()` to allow people to customize the Result if necessary
* move the inner part of the `for row in dataset.dict:` to a new `import_row()` method that would allow people to customize what happens before and after the row import is attempted
* add a `report_error()` and a `report_row_result()` to hold the code that appends the results to the result, once again providing an additional extension point for people that need additional reporting

issue_title: index.add() fails with "maximum recursion depth exceeded"
issue_body:
Here's how it looks:

> Traceback (most recent call last):
>   [...]
>   File "/go/ems/emsppi/dev/plspu/git_release_import/git_release_import.py", line 914, in import_patch
>     self.repo.index.add(files_to_add, fprogress=fprogress)
>   File "/go/ems/emsppi/dev/plspu/git_release_import/ext/git/index/base.py", line 726, in add
>     entries_added.extend(self._entries_for_paths(paths, path_rewriter, fprogress, entries))
>   File "/go/ems/emsppi/dev/plspu/git_release_import/ext/git/util.py", line 54, in wrapper
>     return func(self, *args, **kwargs)
>   File "/go/ems/emsppi/dev/plspu/git_release_import/ext/git/index/util.py", line 84, in set_git_working_dir
>     return func(self, *args, **kwargs)
>   File "/go/ems/emsppi/dev/plspu/git_release_import/ext/git/index/base.py", line 611, in _entries_for_paths
>     for filepath in self._iter_expand_paths(paths):
>   File "/go/ems/emsppi/dev/plspu/git_release_import/ext/git/index/base.py", line 372, in _iter_expand_paths
>     for f in self._iter_expand_paths(glob.glob(abs_path)):
>   [...]
>   File "/go/ems/emsppi/dev/plspu/git_release_import/ext/git/index/base.py", line 372, in _iter_expand_paths
>     for f in self._iter_expand_paths(glob.glob(abs_path)):
>   File "/gow/ems/emsppi/local/lib/python2.7/glob.py", line 27, in glob
>     return list(iglob(pathname))
>   File "/gow/ems/emsppi/local/lib/python2.7/glob.py", line 64, in iglob
>     for name in glob_in_dir(dirname, basename):
>   File "/gow/ems/emsppi/local/lib/python2.7/glob.py", line 82, in glob1
>     names = filter(lambda x: x[0] != '.', names)
>   File "/gow/ems/emsppi/local/lib/python2.7/glob.py", line 82, in <lambda>
>     names = filter(lambda x: x[0] != '.', names)
> RuntimeError: maximum recursion depth exceeded in cmp
>
> the files being added contained a file  "[.exe" amongs them

Amongst the `files_to_add` there was a file called `[.exe` which I believe was the cause of the problem.

Below I attach a patch to `index/base.py` which is a proposal to resolve the issue and which fixed the problem for me. 

```diff
--- a/git/index/base.py
+++ b/git/index/base.py
@@ -369,9 +369,17 @@ class IndexFile(LazyMixin, diff.Diffable, Serializable):

             # resolve globs if possible
             if '?' in path or '*' in path or '[' in path:
-                for f in self._iter_expand_paths(glob.glob(abs_path)):
-                    yield f.replace(rs, '')
-                continue
+                resolved_paths = glob.glob(abs_path)
+                # not abs_path in resolved_paths:
+                #   A glob() resolving to the same path we are feeding it with
+                #   is a glob() that failed to resolve. If we continued calling
+                #   ourselves we'd endlessly recurse. If the condition below
+                #   evaluates to true then we are likely dealing with a file
+                #   whose name contains wildcard characters.
+                if not abs_path in resolved_paths:
+                    for f in self._iter_expand_paths(glob.glob(abs_path)):
+                        yield f.replace(rs, '')
+                    continue
             # END glob handling
             try:
                 for root, dirs, files in os.walk(abs_path, onerror=raise_exc):

```
I am using GitPython v1.0.1.

issue_title: Support for categorical/discrete colour maps in map.geo_json()
issue_body:
The colour mapping when generating choropleth style maps is based around d3.js colour brewer sequential scales.

When generating thematic maps based on a categorical variable (such as election prediction or outcomes by party), it would be convenient to be able to pass in a colour map as a dict that maps {'colvar1':color1, 'colvar2':color2, ..., 'colvarN':colorN}

One option would be to support colorbrewer qualitative scales, though it would also be useful to be able to define color maps for cases where conventional colorings are used (as for political parties, for example).

I can't offhand see how to do that currently?

issue_title: `ratio` should allow to specify which class to target when resampling
issue_body:
**TomekLinks** and **EditedNearestNeighbours** only remove samples form the majority class. However both methods are often used rather for data cleaning (removing samples form both classes) but undersampling (only removing samples form the majority class). Thus **SMOTETomek** and **SMOTEENN** are not implemented as proposed by [Batista, Prati and Monard (2004)](http://sci2s.ugr.es/keel/dataset/includes/catImbFiles/2004-Batista-SIGKDD.pdf), because they use TomekLinks and ENN for removing samples from the majority and the minority class.

It would be great to have a parameter that lets you choose whether to remove samples from both classes or only from the majority class.

issue_title: Use "Embedded in another view" with helpers and/or with "Implicit optional callback"
issue_body:
"Embedded in another view" + "Implicit optional callback":
Views.py
```python
class DataTableView(DatatableView):
    model = Agent 
    datatable_options = {
        'columns': [
            'firstname',
            'lastname',
            ("Certification", 'agent__certifications', 'get_agent_certifications'),
        ]
        }

    def get_agent_certifications(self, instance, *args, **kwargs):
        return ", ".join([Certification.long_name for Certification in instance.certifications.all()])


class SelectionAgentSearchView(LoginRequiredMixin, FormView):
    form_class = SearchForm
    template_name = 'client/datatable.html'
    success_url = 'client/datatable.html'


    def get_context_data(self, **kwargs):
        context = super(SelectionAgentSearchView, self).get_context_data(**kwargs)
        datatable_view = DataTableView()
        # Init Datatables parameters
        model = datatable_view.model
        options = datatable_view.get_datatable_options()
        # Retrieve the selection ID from the url to join with agents
        selectionid = self.kwargs['selectionid']
        datatable = get_datatable_structure(reverse('clients:agents_list'), options, model=model)
        context['datatable'] = datatable
        return context


def agents_list(request):
    if request.method == 'GET':
        agents = Agent.objects.filter().values_list(
            'firstname',
            'lastname',
            '', # Here should be the datas returned by implicit callback
    agents = {"data": list(agents)}
    return JsonResponse(agents, safe=False)
```
Of course, there is no datas in "Certification" column.
I think the way of doings things is putting the implicit callback ```def get_agent_certifications(self, instance, *args, **kwargs):
        return ", ".join([Certification.long_name for Certification in instance.certifications.all()])``` if ```agents_list``` function. Can you confirm or infirm please ?
